source: Live session 2026-06-21: every cheap-tier turn logs 'cerebras error: 404 - Model llama-3.3-70b does not exist or you do not have access to it' then 'cerebras brownout for 300s'.
title: Cerebras model id is invalid (404) — cheap tier brownout-loops on every turn
current_state: cerebras_model defaults to 'llama-3.3-70b' (core/llm_router.py:99, core/providers/cerebras.py:25). Cerebras' API returns 404 model_not_found for this id, so the cheap tier's first provider hard-fails EVERY call and is re-browned every 5 min — exactly the always-browned-out pattern we fixed for Gemini in T-212. normie (tier=cheap) eats a failed Cerebras attempt on every turn before falling through.
target_state: Cerebras uses a model id the account actually serves, and the id is env-overridable (CEREBRAS_MODEL) so a future rename is a config change, not a code change — mirroring the GEMINI_MODEL fix from T-212. The cheap tier stops logging a 404 + brownout on every turn.
migration_plan: 1. Probe the live Cerebras account for valid model ids (a quick models-list or a known-good id like 'llama-3.3-70b' vs 'llama3.1-70b' vs 'llama-4-scout'); pick one the key can access.
2. core/providers/cerebras.py __init__: read os.environ.get('CEREBRAS_MODEL') or model (same pattern as GeminiProvider in T-212). Update the default to the verified id.
3. core/llm_router.py:99: update the cerebras_model default to match.

4. Optional hygiene: a persistent config 404 currently re-fails every 5 min — consider treating a 404 model_not_found as a longer/permanent brownout for that provider until restart (don't retry a config error every 5 min). Flag only; not required to unblock.
5. Tests: testing/test_cerebras_model_config.py (offline, stub the client) — default model is not the broken id; CEREBRAS_MODEL env override wins; router default matches provider default.
risk_notes: Cerebras free-tier model ids change/rotate (just like Gemini's). The env override is the durable fix; the default just needs to be currently-valid. Verify against the real key before closing — this is a live-API correctness bug, not a pure unit-test fix.
